mappings.rationaleAI: Genetics as the scientific study of genes, variation, and heredity broadly relevant to heredity in humans (A), the biological basis of genetic reproduction (A.1), and applications of human genetics (A.3).
Heredity as genetic information transmission from parents to offspring through the biological basis of human genetic reproduction (A.1).
Natural selection driving differential fitness underlying aspects of human physical diversity (B.1).
Evolutionary change in heritable characteristics producing the nature and origin of human physical variation (B.1).
Human evolutionary divergence producing aspects of human diversity and physical variation (B.1).
Sexual reproduction as the mechanism of genetic reproduction providing the biological basis of human heredity (A.1).
Sexual reproduction enabling genetic recombination as the biological basis of human genetic reproduction (A.1).
Racism and its connection to assertions of racial superiority based on heritable differences, directly relevant to measures of race (B.2) and historical racial typology.
Homo sapiens covering anatomy, genetics, and biological variation relevant to aspects of human diversity (B.1).
Biological determinants including genetic factors and prenatal hormone exposure relevant to inheritance of behavioral traits (A.2) and specific behavioral traits affected by inheritance (A.4).

Personality traits linked to life outcomes and influenced by developmental biology and environmental factors, relevant to inheritance of behavioral traits (A.2) and specific traits affected by inheritance (A.4).
Male biological characteristics including XY chromosomal inheritance and secondary sexual characteristics relevant to aspects of human diversity (B.1) and old measures of physical variation (B.2.a).
Discrimination based on race and physical characteristics connected to measures of race and the social consequences of racial classification (B.2).
Mental health risk factors spanning biological dimensions relevant to genetic explanations for abnormalities through chromosome variations and mutation (A.2.b).
Learning shaped by genetics and epigenetics as a specific behavioral trait affected by inheritance (A.4).
